description: ALPHA: subject to change. A declaration of a performed DJ set. A set is an ordered artifact authored by the PERFORMER, in the performer's repository. It is not a listening record. Tracks are embedded by value as social.skaists.alpha.performance.defs#playRef; a set record MUST NOT reference an fm.teal.alpha.feed.play record by URI, because teal plays are consumption records living in a listener's repo and carry no back-reference.

startedAt.description: RFC 3339 time the first track was brought in. Origin for every cueTime in items.

endedAt.description: RFC 3339 time the last track was taken out.

items.description: The tracks, in performance order. Order in this array is advisory; the normative order is the position field. See invariant SET-1.

setKind.description: What kind of artifact this is. Open vocabulary; unrecognized values MUST be preserved verbatim and rendered as unknown, never coerced to 'live'. Orthogonal to performer agency, which is declared per-performer on #performer.kind.

supersedes.description: The prior revision of THIS set, in THIS repository, which this record replaces. CONTENT-PINNED, unlike eventUri: the cid is local to the author's own repo, costs no network round-trip to obtain, and the claim being made is explicitly about the prior record's CONTENT. Sets are append-only. The superseded record MUST NOT be deleted or tombstoned. See invariants SET-9 and SET-10.

performers.description: Ordered for b2b sets. When absent, the repository owner is the sole performer.

venue.description: The venue, embedded by value. LOAD-BEARING. A set is complete and verifiable without eventRef.

eventUri.description: Unpinned reference to a community.lexicon.calendar.event record, typically in the promoter's repository. Deliberately NOT a com.atproto.repo.strongRef: this record asserts PRESENCE, not description. The performer played at that party; the party's name, times, and status are the promoter's to revise, and revisions do not falsify the performer's attestation. ENRICHMENT ONLY: a set with a dangling or unresolvable eventUri remains a valid set. See invariant SET-8.

setDiscriminant.description: Distinguishing information for the set, e.g. 'Opening', 'B2B', 'Closing', 'Sunrise'. Convention borrowed from fm.teal.alpha.feed.play#trackDiscriminant.

recordingUrl.description: A recording of the set, if one exists and the performer chooses to publish it.

submissionClientAgent.description: Format `<app-identifier>/<version> (<kernel/OS-base>; <platform/OS-version>; <device-model>)`. Only app-identifier and version are required. Reverse-DNS app-identifier recommended. Defaults to 'manual/unknown'.
